whatIsMissing: Government support is not enough and is not consistent. Health services are limited, and many people still struggle to get proper treatment. There are not enough toilets in the community, and the existing ones are not well maintained or clean. Also, services are often far away, and many people cannot easily access them when needed.
whyUrgent: because poor healthcare and lack of proper toilets are affecting people’s health every day. Many people get sick easily, and there is a risk of diseases spreading in the community. It is also difficult for women and children especially, and it affects daily life and dignity.

Building more toilets and improving access to medical care would greatly improve living conditions. Awareness programs and regular support from government or NGOs would also help a lot.

problemMost: Drug or substance abuse and gender based violence
whatIsMissing: Current government support is not strong enough to fully address drug abuse and gender-based violence. Awareness programs are limited, and many young people are not reached. There is also poor enforcement of laws, and victims often do not get proper protection or counseling. Community support systems are weak, and many cases are not reported because of fear or stigma.
topPriorityText: Drug or substance abuse and gender based violence
whyUrgent: because drug and substance abuse is increasing among young people, leading to violence, school dropouts, and unsafe communities. Gender-based violence is also affecting the safety and wellbeing of women and girls. If not addressed quickly, it will continue to harm families and the future of young people in the community.
additionalComments: We needs more awareness programs, especially for young people, to educate them about the dangers of drug abuse and gender-based violence. There should also be stronger support systems for victims, including counseling services and safe spaces. More involvement from government, NGOs, and community leaders would help reduce these problems. Creating job opportunities and skills training for youth could also reduce drug use and improve behavior in the community.

topPriorityText: The top priority problem that should be solved first is youth unemployment and lack of digital skills opportunities.
whyUrgent: This problem is urgent because a large number of young people in our community are finishing school but are unable to find jobs or start sustainable businesses. Many do not have access to digital skills training, internet resources, mentorship, or funding opportunities. As a result, talented youth remain idle, underemployed, or forced into low-income informal work.
barriers: Cost and affordability – Many people may assume the program is expensive or may not afford transport, internet, or participation fees.

What would help most is long-term support rather than one-time interventions. Sustainable programs that provide continuous training, mentorship, internet access, and small startup support can create lasting impact.

problemMost:
Gender-Based Violence (GBV) affects me the most personally.
It is a serious issue that impacts the safety, health, confidence, and opportunities of women and girls in my community. Many survivors face fear, stigma, and lack of support, which makes it difficult to speak out or seek help. This problem limits education, employment, mental wellbeing, and overall quality of life, making it a major barrier to empowerment and equality.
currentSolutions:
Government efforts
The Ministry of Gender and Children’s Affairs leads national policies, coordination meetings and survivor-centred response systems.
whatIsMissing:
Limited awareness and stigma
Many survivors do not report abuse due to shame, fear, or lack of knowledge about available services.
Harmful cultural norms still normalise violence or blame victims.
whyUrgent: Gender-based violence is urgent because it affects the safety, health, and future of women and girls every day. Many survivors face physical injury, trauma, unwanted pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, and long-term mental health challenges. It also limits girls’ education, reduces women’s ability to work, and keeps families trapped in poverty.
barriers: Lack of trust: Some community members may not trust new programs, especially if they are unfamiliar or fear stigma.
additionalComments: Offer education and awareness programs to reduce stigma and change harmful norms.
